name: Irvine Unified School District
districtFact: For calendar planning, Irvine Unified's most important distinction is that not every student follows the same schedule. IUSD serves more than 38,000 TK-12 students across traditional, year-round, virtual, and specialized programs, so families should confirm the assigned calendar before planning breaks, enrollment steps, childcare, or travel.

about.title: About Irvine Unified School District
about.content: Irvine Unified School District serves TK-12 students in Irvine, California, within Orange County. For calendar planning, IUSD is more complex than a single districtwide date sheet because the district publishes traditional instructional calendars, separate year-round calendars, and program-specific information for virtual, alternative, early childhood, and specialized settings. Irvine's neighborhood assignment model also means families often coordinate the academic calendar with school assignment, school choice, childcare, athletics, arts, enrichment programs, and campus-specific communications.

about.title: How to Use This IUSD Calendar
about.content: Use this page for traditional-school first day, last day, Thanksgiving recess, winter recess, spring recess, staff development days, teacher work days, and major holidays. Then confirm whether your school follows the Traditional Calendar or a Year Round Calendar on the official IUSD district school calendars page. Bell schedules, school choice windows, enrollment appointments, childcare programs, orientation dates, athletics, performances, and site-specific minimum days should be checked through IUSD enrollment resources and individual school communications.

planningTips.title: Planning Tips for Irvine Families
content: Confirm whether your school follows the Traditional Calendar or a Year Round Calendar before booking travel, camps, or recurring childcare.
Check school assignment and enrollment steps before the first day if your family is new to Irvine, moving within IUSD, or using school choice.
IUSD lists some teacher work days by grade span, including high-school-only and PK-8 floating teacher work days. Check your student's level before assuming a date applies districtwide.
Use Irvine childcare, summer camp, athletics, and enrichment calendars alongside the IUSD academic calendar because registration windows can open before the next school year begins.
Add staff development days and teacher/student holidays to family calendars because they can create single-day childcare needs outside the major recess weeks.
Use the official IUSD district school calendars page for revised PDFs and school-year updates.

compareIntro: Compare the traditional calendars for IUSD and nearby California districts. Dates shown include the first day, last day, spring break, instructional days, major breaks, calendar type, district size, and county when source data is available.

districtFaqs.q: Which IUSD calendar does this page summarize?
districtFaqs.a: This page summarizes the IUSD Traditional Instructional Calendar. IUSD also publishes Year Round Calendar PDFs for applicable schools, which are linked on the year pages when available.

districtFaqs.q: Does every Irvine Unified school follow the same calendar?
districtFaqs.a: No. IUSD publishes both traditional and year-round calendars. Families should confirm the assigned calendar with their school before relying on any break or no-school date.

districtFaqs.q: Are IUSD teacher work days no-school days for every student?
districtFaqs.a: Not always. Some IUSD teacher work days are grade-span specific, such as high-school-only teacher work days or PK-8 floating teacher work days. This page labels those dates when the official calendar makes that distinction.

districtFaqs.q: Where should families verify last-minute IUSD calendar changes?
districtFaqs.a: Use the official Irvine Unified district school calendars page and school-level announcements for final confirmation. MySchoolDates is an independent reference and is not affiliated with IUSD.

districtFaqs.q: Does the IUSD calendar determine school assignment?
districtFaqs.a: No. School assignment is based on the student's residential address, attendance boundaries, and IUSD enrollment rules. The calendar tells families when schools are in session, but enrollment and school assignment should be verified through IUSD enrollment resources.

districtFaqs.q: Does IUSD school choice use the same dates as the academic calendar?
districtFaqs.a: Not exactly. School choice and intradistrict transfer windows are separate administrative timelines. Families should check IUSD school choice information in addition to the academic calendar before making school-placement or childcare plans.

districtFaqs.q: Do IUSD virtual academy or independent study students use this calendar?
districtFaqs.a: Some dates may align with the district calendar, but virtual academy, independent study, and specialized programs can have program-specific requirements. Families should confirm dates with the program or school before relying on the traditional calendar alone.

districtFaqs.q: Where can Irvine families find childcare or after-school options?
districtFaqs.a: IUSD enrollment resources point families to childcare information, including City of Irvine childcare resources and school-site options. Those programs may have registration deadlines or operating days that differ from the academic calendar.

customSections.label: Which IUSD Calendar Applies to Your Child?
customSections.content: Irvine calendar planning starts with one practical question: whether your child follows the Traditional Instructional Calendar, a Year Round Calendar, or a program-specific schedule. IUSD also runs enrollment, school assignment, and school choice processes on separate timelines, so the academic calendar should not be treated as the only planning document.

groups.label: How to confirm the right calendar
items: Traditional Instructional Calendar: use for most IUSD traditional-school first day, last day, holidays, recesses, staff development days, and teacher work days.
Year Round Calendar: use for schools or programs assigned to a year-round schedule when IUSD publishes the separate year-round PDF for that school year.
Grade-span work days: check whether a teacher work day applies to PK-8, high school, or all students before planning childcare for siblings.
School assignment and enrollment: verify through IUSD enrollment resources because residential assignment, documentation, and appointments are not controlled by the academic calendar.
School choice or intradistrict transfer: check the school choice timeline separately because choice windows can open or close outside the main break schedule.
Virtual academy, independent study, and specialized programs: confirm program calendars directly when a student does not follow the standard traditional-school pattern.

customSections.label: Parent Planning Checklist
customSections.content: Use the IUSD academic calendar together with enrollment, school choice, childcare, and enrichment resources before making fixed family plans. These checkpoints are especially useful for families new to Irvine, families comparing traditional and year-round schools, and households coordinating siblings in different grade spans.

groups.label: Calendar-linked family checkpoints
items: Enrollment and school assignment: confirm the assigned school, required documents, immunization steps, and any appointment timing before the first instructional day.
School choice: check elementary and secondary school choice windows separately from the academic calendar because those deadlines can fall outside major breaks.
Childcare and camps: review City of Irvine childcare, school-site programs, summer camp, athletics, arts, tutoring, and enrichment calendars before teacher work days and long recesses.
Bell schedules and site events: use individual school communications for orientation, minimum days, performances, athletics, and campus-specific events.
Moving within Irvine: pair calendar dates with boundary information, school assignment, and school-specific communications before making housing or commute decisions.

customSections.label: Three-Year IUSD Calendar History
customSections.content: Recent Irvine Unified traditional calendars show why families should use the specific year page instead of assuming the same dates repeat every year. First day, last day, Spring Recess placement, staff development days, and grade-span teacher work days can shift from one school year to the next.

groups.label: Recent year notes
items: 2025-2026: IUSD starts August 19, 2025 and ends June 5, 2026, with Spring Recess scheduled March 30-April 3, 2026.
2026-2027: IUSD starts August 18, 2026 and ends June 4, 2027, with Spring Recess scheduled March 29-April 2, 2027.
2027-2028: IUSD starts August 18, 2027 and ends June 2, 2028, with Spring Recess scheduled April 3-7, 2028.
Across the three traditional calendars, IUSD lists 180 student instructional days and uses separate grade-span teacher work day labels that families should check before planning childcare.
